Phos..personality: The Phosphorus constitution is a delicate, slender, rapidly emaciating individual who is vehement and irascible, with an internal state of turmoil. They are highly sensitive to all external impressions—odors, noises, touch—and prone to great excitability that can reach ecstasy or even clairvoyance. They crave company, are worse when alone or in the dark, and desire cold drinks and refreshing things.
Phos..mental_state: The mind oscillates between overactivity with wild thoughts, violent imaginations, and inability to sustain mental effort, and extreme passivity with loss of memory, sluggish thinking, and stupor. There is great prostration of mind after even slight mental exertion, with reflecting bringing on headaches and difficult breathing. The mental state can progress from anxiety and fearfulness through hypochondriasis to the borderland of insanity, imbecility, and idiocy.
Phos..emotional_pattern: Fear is the dominant emotional theme—fear at twilight, fear when alone, fear of death, fear during thunderstorms, fear of apoplexy—with anxiety seeming to originate from the pit of the stomach. There is a marked oscillation between emotional extremes: from weeping, sadness, and deep indifference to loved ones and surroundings, to violent loquacious delirium and maniacal fury. Gloomy forebodings and apprehensiveness pervade, alternating with periods of complete apathy where everything looks dark and the patient is weary of life.
